title: ACS community-district covariates — provenance sidecar
description: Per-district demographic covariates derived from the American Community Survey 5-year estimates. Used by step 05 (`05_spatial_analysis.py`) to populate per-district population weights in the spatial Moran's I and Theil index calculations. This sidecar is read by humans and release auditors; scripts load `demographics.csv` directly. If a column here drifts from the CSV header, the CSV is canonical.
geography: NYC community district (59 units; the sample ships 51 with matched rodent-complaints coverage)

columns.name: unit_id
columns.description: Community district identifier matching nyc-geo-toolkit's canonical labelling, e.g. 'BRONX 04'.

columns.name: population
columns.description: Total resident population (ACS table B01003_001E, 5-year estimates).

columns.name: pct_nonwhite
columns.description: Fraction of residents identifying as other than 'White alone'. Derived from table B02001. Range [0, 1].

columns.name: log_median_income
columns.description: Natural log of median household income in 2023 USD. Derived from B19013_001E. Log-transformed to stabilize variance for panel regression.

columns.name: pct_renter
columns.description: Fraction of occupied housing units that are renter-occupied (not owner-occupied). Derived from table B25003. Range [0, 1].

how_to_regenerate: The values were assembled manually from the ACS tables above using data.census.gov; no automated fetch pipeline ships with this case study. If you need a different vintage or set of covariates, fetch the corresponding tables from the Census API and overwrite this CSV.
notes: `log_median_income` uses natural log. Back-transform with math.exp() for dollar values.
These covariates are strictly time-invariant in this case study; do not pair with a pre/post-period panel that needs time-varying demographics.
